risks.risk: Ensemble disagreement on power-scarcity thesis: Altimeter exited while Light Street holds. If Altimeter's exit reflects discovery that power supply expansion (competing fuel cells, geothermal, nuclear) will alleviate the binding constraint faster than the SA LP framing assumes, the consumption-model justification for 22.79% sizing loses its anchor.
risks.grounding_citation: Thorstad's formal-theory frame distinguishes risk channels; consumption-as-risk-driver applies cleanly to power infrastructure, but ONLY if the constraint actually persists through the position horizon. Aschenbrenner's 100GW binding-constraint framing (SA LP source artifact tier 1) is the load-bearing assumption; off-thesis exits may signal its falsification.

risks.risk: Consumption model assumes risk is monotonic in demand: more AI -> more electricity demand -> higher risk if supply lags. But if power does scale to meet demand (via Bloom or competitors), binding-constraint assumptions ease and multiples may compress despite consumption growth. The formal model does not address the 'supply wins' scenario.
risks.grounding_citation: Trammell-Aschenbrenner ERAG framework: consumption-as-risk-driver assumes Kuznets-curve investment dynamics. For power infrastructure, this implies risk peaks when supply is scarce relative to demand, then falls as capacity expands. BE's position sizing (22.79% of long book) assumes the peak-scarcity phase persists through 2026-2028; relief of scarcity would invalidate that assumption.

adjustment_rationale: Bloom Energy sits in the consumption-risk-channel domain (electricity demand from AI clusters), and the Trammell-Aschenbrenner ERAG formal model with consumption-as-risk-driver applies cleanly to power infrastructure positioning. The model does justify holding this position at the 2.28% of total portfolio weight (22.79% of long book post-trim). The ensemble disagreement (Altimeter exit vs Light Street hold) is an information signal worth monitoring — it may reflect off-thesis discovery about supply competition or Bloom's product durability — but it does not constitute a formal-theoretic critique of the model itself. The consumption model is fit-for-purpose here; my critique lane remains closed. Both flagged risks are low-severity and do not aggregate to a reduction. Hold the position at target weight.
